original.text: The consent mentioned in this section may be express or implied, and may be given either by the person in possession, or by any person having for that purpose authority either express or implied.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 17: OF OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY
Section 303: Theft.
Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person’s consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
The consent mentioned in this section may be express or implied, and may be given either by the person in possession, or by any person having for that purpose authority either express or implied.

original.text: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person’s consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 17: OF OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY
Section 303: Theft.
Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person’s consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.

original.text: A thing so long as it is attached to the earth, not being movable property, is not the subject of theft; but it becomes capable of being the subject of theft as soon as it is severed from the earth.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 17: OF OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY
Section 303: Theft.
Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person’s consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
A thing so long as it is attached to the earth, not being movable property, is not the subject of theft; but it becomes capable of being the subject of theft as soon as it is severed from the earth.

original.text: A moving effected by the same act which affects the severance may be a theft.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 17: OF OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY
Section 303: Theft.
Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person’s consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
A moving effected by the same act which affects the severance may be a theft.

original.text: A takes an article belonging to Z out of Z’s possession without Z’s consent, with the intention of keeping it until he obtains money from Z as a reward for its restoration. Here A takes dishonestly; A has therefore committed theft.
original.derived_context:
Act: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023
Chapter 17: OF OFFENCES AGAINST PROPERTY
Section 303: Theft.
Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person’s consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
A takes an article belonging to Z out of Z’s possession without Z’s consent, with the intention of keeping it until he obtains money from Z as a reward for its restoration. Here A takes dishonestly; A has therefore committed theft.
